blockingIssues.description: `orchestrator.unregisterScoop()` emits `onStatusChange(jid, 'ready')` before removing bridge scoops from orchestrator state, so both scoop-panel refresh paths can keep rendering a cleaned-up bridge scoop until a later refresh.

`RestrictedFS.readDirSync()` delegates strictly allowed paths straight to `VirtualFS.readDirSync()`, which follows symlinked directory paths; shell commands like `ls`/`find` can still enumerate out-of-sandbox contents through symlinked directories.

appliedUpdates.description: Corrected `.factory/library/architecture.md` to record the remaining `RestrictedFS.readDirSync()` symlinked-directory ACL gap instead of treating the sync fast path as equivalent to async `readDir()`.

Corrected `.factory/library/architecture.md` to note that bridge-scoop cleanup currently signals `onStatusChange('ready')` before removing the scoop from orchestrator state, which can leave ghost scoop rows until a later refresh.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that user-visible UI fixes still require manual CDP/agent-browser verification even when the feature's fulfilled assertions are Vitest-based, and workers who skip that check must mark `followedProcedure: false`.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature `fix-agent-scoop-visibility` changed scoop-panel visibility/status behavior but its handoff recorded no interactive checks and still marked `followedProcedure=true`, despite the skill's manual-validation requirement.
